One case has also been confirmed in the capital Kinshasa. The current strain is caused by the Bundibugyo virus, for which no approved drugs or vaccines exist.

The outbreak has crossed borders. Two confirmed cases have been reported in neighboring Uganda, and a case has been confirmed in Goma. WHO officials warned the true number of infections is likely higher than reported, with significant risk of wider local and regional spread.

The WHO cited the ongoing security situation, high population movement, urban locations, and large numbers of informal health clinics as factors raising the risk. Neighboring countries are considered high risk due to trade and travel links.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Congolese communities facing a deadly Ebola outbreak with no approved treatment and risk of wider regional spread

events.title: Civil Unrest in Tunisia 05/17/2026 — Tunis
events.body:
Hundreds of people marched through Tunisia's capital to protest a deepening economic crisis and what demonstrators describe as an increasing crackdown on political dissent. The protesters voiced opposition to what they see as the erosion of freedoms gained after the 2011 revolution.

The country's president faces accusations of weakening the democratic institutions that emerged from that period. Political arrests have added to public frustration alongside the ongoing economic difficulties.

The demonstrations reflect growing tension between the government and those who fear a reversal of the political gains made over the past decade. Ordinary Tunisians continue to feel the pressure of both financial hardship and a shrinking space for public opposition.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Tunisian citizens amid deepening economic hardship and rising restrictions on political freedoms

events.title: Armed Conflict in Iran 05/17/2026 — Zawtar al-Sharqiyah
events.body:
Iran announced it will soon reveal a plan to charge tolls on traffic passing through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global shipping. A senior Iranian official stated the country will no longer allow military equipment from "enemy" nations to pass through the strait. The United States president warned Iran it would face a "very bad time" if a peace deal is not reached soon.

Separately, Israeli airstrikes continued in southern Lebanon, with an attack reported on the town of Zawtar al-Sharqiyah. Israel's bombardment of the region has been described as relentless, raising ongoing concerns for civilians living in affected communities.

The two situations together reflect a wider pattern of tension across the Middle East, with pressure building both on land in Lebanon and over contested waters at the Strait of Hormuz.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Lebanon enduring ongoing Israeli airstrikes amid escalating regional tensions

events.title: Armed Conflict in Russia 05/17/2026 — Moscow region
events.body:
Ukraine launched what Russian officials called one of the largest drone attacks of the war, sending waves of drones toward Moscow and other regions overnight. At least five people were killed — including a foreign worker and a woman whose home was struck in Khimki, north of Moscow, and two men in the Mytishchi district. One more person died in Belgorod, near the Ukrainian border. Russia's defense ministry said it intercepted 556 drones across 14 regions, as well as over annexed Crimea and nearby seas. Debris from the attacks fell on the grounds of Moscow's largest airport, though no damage was reported there.

Ukraine's security service said its forces struck an oil refinery and two oil-pumping stations in the Moscow region. Ukrainian officials said the attacks were a direct response to ongoing Russian strikes on Ukrainian cities.

Meanwhile, Russian attacks continued inside Ukraine. In the Kharkiv region, 15 settlements were targeted over 24 hours, wounding seven people. In the Kherson region, a man was killed when a Russian drone dropped explosives on a village.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Russia and Ukraine caught in escalating large-scale drone and missile exchanges across both countries

events.title: Armed Conflict in Iran 02/28/2026 — Minab
events.body:
A senior US military commander told a Senate hearing this week that his command has not investigated widely reported claims that US airstrikes have hit schools and hospitals across Iran. The head of US Central Command said there is "no indication" that 22 schools were struck, despite reports from the Iranian Red Crescent Society claiming that attacks on universities and schools have killed at least 60 students and 10 staff members.

The commander acknowledged only one active civilian casualty investigation out of more than 13,000 munitions used in strikes on Iran. That investigation relates to a February 28 strike on a girls' elementary school in Minab, where reports say 156 students and staff were killed and 95 others wounded. US officials initially denied responsibility, but journalistic investigations and a preliminary Pentagon review have pointed to US involvement.

A senator pressed the commander repeatedly on why the allegations had not been investigated, especially given his stated commitment to minimizing civilian harm. The commander offered no clear explanation for the lack of inquiries.
events.source_title: antiwar.com
events.prayer: Students, teachers, and civilians in Iran killed and wounded amid ongoing airstrikes targeting populated areas including schools

events.title: Displacement in Sudan — Sudan
events.body:
Nearly 20 million people across Sudan are facing acute hunger, according to a warning issued by United Nations agencies. More than 800,000 children are at risk of severe malnutrition this year alone. Parts of the country are being described as edging closer to famine conditions.

The crisis is being driven by ongoing civil war, the mass displacement of civilians, and the collapse of both food supply and health systems. What was already one of the world's worst humanitarian situations continues to worsen as fighting disrupts the ability of aid organizations to respond.

Families who have fled their homes face the greatest danger, with little access to food, clean water, or medical care. The combination of conflict and hunger is pushing many communities past what they can survive without outside help.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Nearly 20 million people in Sudan facing acute hunger, mass displacement, and deepening famine conditions amid civil war

events.title: Armed Conflict in Afghanistan 03/16/2026 — Kabul
events.body:
At least 269 people were killed on March 16, 2026, when an airstrike hit the Omid Drug Rehabilitation Hospital in Kabul. Pakistan told the BBC it had targeted "military and terrorist infrastructure," but the strike hit a facility where civilians were receiving treatment for drug addiction.

The BBC spoke with families of more than 30 victims, including relatives of patients in recovery and hospital staff, all of whom rejected Pakistan's account of the strike. The United Nations was granted access to the site and, along with BBC journalists on the ground in the hours after the attack, confirmed that civilians undergoing treatment were killed.

The full toll on those inside the facility — patients, staff, and others — remains one of the deadliest single strikes on Afghan soil in recent years, leaving many families without answers about why their loved ones were targeted.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Hundreds of civilians in Afghanistan killed when an airstrike destroyed a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ukraine 05/16/2025 — Kyiv
events.body:
A Russian cruise missile struck a nine-storey apartment building in southeastern Kyiv, killing 24 people including three girls aged 12 and 15. Rescue workers spent 28 hours searching the rubble before ending their operation. Among those killed were postal workers, a former hockey player, a kindergarten teacher, and an English teacher. A 12-year-old girl who lost her father earlier in the war also died, along with her older sister. Kyiv held a day of mourning, and flowers were laid at the site.

On the same day, Russian officials reported that Ukrainian drone strikes hit the city of Ryazan, southeast of Moscow, killing four people including a child and wounding 28 others. A Ukrainian drone commander said the attack targeted Ryazan's oil refinery, one of Russia's largest.

Also on Friday, Russia and Ukraine exchanged 205 prisoners of war each, the first stage of a planned 1,000-person swap brokered by the United States and United Arab Emirates. The exchange followed a three-day ceasefire earlier in May that both sides said was repeatedly violated, and which ended with a wave of large-scale Russian strikes across Ukraine.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Ukraine grieving after a deadly missile strike on a residential apartment building killed 24 people

events.title: Armed Conflict in Israel — Gaza City
events.body:
An Israeli air strike on a residential building in central Gaza City killed a senior Hamas military commander on Friday, according to Hamas and Israeli officials. Eyewitnesses told the BBC that three missiles struck the apartment block from two directions, sparking a large fire. Rescue teams struggled to reach the wounded. A second strike hit a car about a kilometre and a half away, killing three people. Witnesses said the vehicle may have been carrying the wounded commander as he fled.

A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas came into effect on 10 October, but Israel has continued to carry out strikes across Gaza since then. Israel says it has the right to target Hamas members, while Hamas accuses Israel of violating the ceasefire and attacking civilians. The two sides remain at a standoff over disarmament, with Hamas reactivating its police force and reasserting control in parts of Gaza.

Since the ceasefire began, 857 people have been killed in Gaza, according to the Hamas-run health ministry. Broader peace talks remain deadlocked.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: civilians living near military targets in Gaza City, and for progress in stalled peace efforts

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon — Harouf
events.body:
An Israeli air strike on the town of Harouf in southern Lebanon killed six people, including three paramedics, according to Lebanon's health ministry. A fourth paramedic was critically injured when a civil defence centre was hit. Days earlier, on Wednesday, air strikes killed 22 people across the south, including eight children.

Despite a ceasefire announced by US President Donald Trump in April, both sides have continued trading fire almost daily. The US announced a 45-day extension of the truce after two days of negotiations in Washington, with a military track of talks set to begin at the Pentagon on May 29.

More than one million people — roughly one in five Lebanese — have been displaced since the conflict began, most from the south, the Bekaa Valley, and southern Beirut. At least 2,896 people have been killed in Lebanon since fighting started, according to the health ministry.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians and paramedics in Lebanon killed amid ongoing strikes despite a fragile ceasefire

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nigeria — Metele, Borno state
events.body:
Nigeria and the United States carried out a joint operation that killed a senior Islamic State (IS) leader, along with several of his associates, at a compound in Metele, Borno state, in northeastern Nigeria. The operation began shortly after midnight on a Saturday, following months of intelligence gathering. Officials said no casualties were suffered during the strike.

The man killed was described by US officials as among the most senior figures in the global IS hierarchy and was linked to attacks on civilians across West Africa and the Sahel, including the 2018 kidnapping of more than 100 schoolgirls in Dapchi, Nigeria. He had previously been a senior commander within Boko Haram before aligning with IS.

The Lake Chad Basin, where the strike took place, has long been a stronghold for extremist groups. IS now carries out roughly 90% of its attacks in sub-Saharan Africa, with its Nigeria-based branch considered the most active. Both governments called the operation a sign of growing military cooperation between the two countries.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians across Nigeria facing continued extremist violence from active Islamic State networks in the Lake Chad region

events.title: Displacement in Somalia — Somalia
events.body:
United Nations aid teams warned on Friday that Somalia faces a real risk of famine, with at least six million people going days at a time without enough food. Nearly two million of those affected are young children described as being at high risk of illness or death.

The warning came as the fallout from the conflict in the Middle East continues to affect humanitarian funding and aid delivery in other parts of the world. Somalia has long struggled with food insecurity, and the current situation appears to be worsening.

With millions of people — including large numbers of vulnerable children — unable to meet basic food needs, the humanitarian situation remains severe. The UN's alert signals that without a significant response, conditions on the ground could deteriorate into full famine.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Six million people in Somalia, including nearly two million young children, facing severe hunger and potential famine

events.title: Armed Conflict in Israel — Gaza, West Bank, Lebanon
events.body:
Civilians across Gaza, the West Bank, and Lebanon are facing deepening hardship as military operations continue and humanitarian conditions worsen, according to UN agencies. Displacement is ongoing, and aid efforts are being strained by the scale of need.

UN reports indicate that the conflict is making it increasingly difficult for relief organizations to reach those who need help most. Food, medical care, and basic services remain limited across the affected areas.

The situation is described as a growing humanitarian crisis, with no immediate signs of relief for those caught in the middle of the fighting.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: civilians in Gaza, the West Bank, and Lebanon who are caught in ongoing violence and struggling to access food, medical care, and safe shelter

events.title: Displacement in Congo — eastern Congo
events.body:
Ongoing fighting in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo has forced millions of people from their homes, including large numbers of children whose education has been disrupted by the conflict.

In response, new schools have been established to serve displaced children in the region. These efforts aim to give young people a sense of normalcy and hope amid the instability, allowing them to continue learning even after being uprooted from their communities.

Despite the difficult conditions, children in these schools are expressing ambitions for their futures, a sign that access to education is making a meaningful difference in their lives. For families scattered by war, schools are becoming one of the few stable anchors available to children growing up in displacement.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Millions displaced in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, including children losing access to stable education amid ongoing conflict

events.title: Armed Conflict in Israel 05/16/2026 — Gaza City
events.body:
At least seven Palestinians were killed when Israeli air strikes hit a residential building and a civilian vehicle in Gaza City on Friday night. The strikes caused casualties in what witnesses described as a populated residential area.

Israeli officials said the strikes were targeting the head of Hamas's armed wing in Gaza. The news outlet reporting the story noted it could not independently verify that claim.

The strikes on a residential building highlight ongoing dangers for civilians living in active conflict zones, where attacks in densely populated areas can affect many people beyond any intended target.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: the families of those killed in these strikes and for the protection of civilians in residential areas

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nigeria 05/16/2026 — Lake Chad Basin
events.body:
A joint U.S.-Nigerian operation killed a senior Islamic State (ISIS) leader and several of his associates in a strike on his compound in the Lake Chad Basin, the presidents of both countries announced. The man, who had been under U.S. sanctions since 2023 and was described as one of the group's most senior global figures, was found hiding in Africa according to U.S. officials.

Both governments called the operation a model of international cooperation against terrorism. Nigeria's president said he hopes more strikes against armed groups will follow, and noted that U.S. forces have been providing intelligence and technical support inside the country in a noncombat role.

Nigeria has faced ongoing violence from armed groups in its northwest and other regions. The Lake Chad Basin area has long been a base of operations for extremist activity affecting multiple countries in the broader region.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Nigeria and the Lake Chad Basin region facing continued threats from ISIS-linked armed groups amid ongoing counterterrorism operations

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon 05/16/2025 — southern Lebanon
events.body:
Lebanon and Israel have agreed to extend a fragile ceasefire by 45 days, pushing past a deadline that fell on Sunday. The extension came after talks in the United States. Despite the agreement, Israeli forces continued to strike towns and villages in southern Lebanon. Lebanese authorities say at least 12 people were killed on Friday, among them three paramedics.

On the diplomatic side, Iran's foreign minister said during a BRICS meeting that his government received signals from the Trump administration showing openness to fresh negotiations over the ongoing conflict. However, he said a deadlock remains around the question of Iran's enriched nuclear material. The US president also floated the idea of Iran pausing its civilian nuclear programme for twenty years if Tehran shows genuine commitment to a broader deal.

Civilians in southern Lebanon remain in a precarious position as fighting continues even while diplomats talk.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanon caught between ongoing Israeli strikes and a fragile ceasefire extension

events.title: Displacement in Palestine — Jabalia
events.body:
In the Jabalia refugee camp in northern Gaza, an 85-year-old man sits beside the ruins of his home. He first became a refugee as a child in 1948, when his family fled their hometown near Beersheba on foot, carrying livestock and supplies, expecting to return in weeks. They never did. The family eventually settled in Jabalia, where they endured tent camps, hunger, cold, and poor sanitation.

Over the following decades, the man worked in construction, built homes, and bought land alongside his brothers — trying to recover what had been lost. Then the war that began in October 2023 destroyed it all again. As an elderly man with limited mobility, he was forced to flee multiple times alongside his aging wife and the families of his late brother's sons.

He describes what he is living through now as worse than anything he witnessed in 1948. "A Nakba at the beginning of my life," he says, "and another Nakba at the end of it." The couple remains in their partially destroyed home, surrounded by rubble.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: an elderly couple and countless others in Gaza who have lost everything, and for all who have been displaced again and again throughout their lifetimes

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon 05/16/2026 — southern Lebanon
events.body:
Despite a ceasefire agreement between Lebanon and Israel being extended for another 45 days following talks in Washington, Israeli air strikes and artillery attacks continued across southern Lebanon on Saturday. Strikes hit at least five villages in the south, and Israeli warplanes attacked the town of Yohmor al-Shaqif. Heavy artillery bombardment also struck Kfar Tebnit, Arnoun, and a road connecting the two towns. The Israeli military said it targeted what it described as Hezbollah infrastructure sites.

At the same time as the ceasefire extension was being announced, Israeli forces issued forced displacement orders for nine villages near Sidon and Nabatieh, as well as for two buildings in the city of Tyre. Residents in the south say the ceasefire announcement has brought little relief, with many expecting further escalation.

Lebanese authorities report that Israeli attacks have killed more than 2,900 people in Lebanon since the conflict began, including over 500 since the truce formally took effect on April 17.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians across southern Lebanon displaced and killed amid ongoing strikes despite an active ceasefire

events.title: Thailand 05/16/2026 — Bangkok
events.body:
At least eight people were killed and 35 others injured on Saturday afternoon when a freight train struck a public bus at an intersection in central Bangkok. The bus appeared to become stuck on the rail crossing after safety barriers came down, and the train struck it at roughly 3:40 p.m. local time. The impact dragged nearby vehicles along the tracks before the bus caught fire. All eight victims were found on board the bus.

Firefighters and emergency crews worked to pull survivors from the wreckage and bring the blaze under control. It is not yet known how many passengers were on the bus in total, and investigators have not confirmed whether the safety barriers functioned properly.

The country's prime minister ordered an investigation into the crash. The incident follows a deadly rail accident earlier this year in which a construction crane fell onto a passenger train northeast of Bangkok, killing at least 28 people. Both incidents have raised public concern about the safety of Thailand's rail network.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families in Thailand grieving eight dead and dozens injured after a deadly train-bus collision

events.title: Thailand 05/16/2026 — Bangkok
events.body:
A freight train collided with a public bus in Bangkok, Thailand's capital, killing at least eight people and injuring 25 others.

The collision was captured on video. No further details about the cause of the crash or the exact location within the city were included in the initial report.

The injured were taken for medical treatment. Residents and commuters in the area would have been directly affected by the incident.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families in Thailand grieving losses and dozens injured after a deadly train and bus collision

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ukraine — Kyiv
events.body:
Russian military strikes hit a civilian area of Kyiv, drawing a strong response from the top United Nations humanitarian official in Ukraine. The UN Humanitarian Coordinator called the attacks a clear violation of international humanitarian law.

The strikes prompted the senior official to speak out publicly, emphasizing that civilian areas must be protected under the rules of war. No further details about casualties or the scale of the damage were included in the available source material.

Residents in Kyiv and surrounding areas continue to face risk from ongoing military strikes targeting populated areas.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Civilians in Ukraine enduring ongoing military strikes on populated residential areas of the capital

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon — Lebanon
events.body:
Israeli attacks on Lebanon have continued even as a ceasefire is nominally in place. The situation has drawn international attention, with the United States hosting a third round of talks between Lebanese and Israeli officials in Washington.

Hezbollah, the Lebanese armed group, has opposed the Washington meeting. Analysts note that Lebanon has endured decades of conflict, and questions remain about whether the current diplomatic process can lead to any lasting arrangement.

The talks reflect ongoing uncertainty about conditions on the ground, where civilians continue to face the effects of military activity despite earlier ceasefire understandings.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians across Lebanon enduring continued military strikes despite an existing ceasefire agreement

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon 05/14/2026 — Tyre
events.body:
A third round of direct talks between Lebanon and Israel began in Washington on Thursday, with higher-level envoys from both countries attending. Lebanese officials hope the two-day meetings will produce a new ceasefire deal and address unresolved issues, including the withdrawal of Israeli forces from southern Lebanon. The current ceasefire agreement is set to expire on Sunday.

Despite the diplomatic activity, fighting has not stopped. UN peacekeepers in Lebanon reported significant aerial and military activity in their operating area, including multiple Israeli air strikes on Wednesday. Residents in southern Lebanon have endured more than two months of ongoing Israeli strikes and artillery fire.

People in southern Lebanon are described as cautiously hopeful but realistic. Hezbollah, which is not part of the talks, has openly rejected the direct negotiations. Lebanese constitutional law also complicates any path toward full normalization with Israel, which residents acknowledge as a significant barrier to a lasting settlement.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Lebanon enduring ongoing strikes as ceasefire talks in Washington near a critical deadline

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ukraine 05/14/2026 — Kherson
events.body:
A United Nations humanitarian convoy delivering aid to Kherson was struck twice by drones while traveling in the area. The convoy had coordinated its route in advance with both Ukrainian and Russian forces, but was attacked anyway. No injuries were reported.

The UN has not said which side carried out the strikes. The incident raises serious concerns about the safety of aid workers and civilians trying to receive humanitarian assistance in the region.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: UN humanitarian convoy in Ukraine struck by drones despite prior coordination, endangering aid workers and civilians

events.title: Civil Unrest in Bolivia 05/14/2026 — La Paz
events.body:
Protesters led by mining groups and rural unions clashed with police in La Paz on Thursday, with small explosions heard as miners set off sticks of dynamite during the demonstrations. Some protesters attempted to breach the presidential palace. The unrest is the latest in weeks of road blockades and demonstrations by miners, farmers, teachers, and rural workers frustrated with Bolivia's severe economic crisis.

The crisis stems from the collapse of Bolivia's natural gas industry. The country once exported large amounts of natural gas, but reserves have shrunk and production has fallen sharply, turning Bolivia into a net energy importer. The result has been soaring inflation, fuel shortages, and hospitals running low on basic supplies like oxygen and medicine.

Protesters are calling on President Rodrigo Paz to resign, just as they had demanded of his predecessor. Officials have refused, with one minister stating the president would not step down. Earlier in the day, government representatives met with a group of miners and said they were open to dialogue on fuel subsidies and welfare benefits.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Bolivian civilians, miners, and workers amid economic collapse, fuel shortages, and hospital supply crises

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon — Saksakiyeh
events.body:
An Israeli airstrike destroyed a building in the southern Lebanese town of Saksakiyeh where a displaced family was sheltering, killing nine people. Relatives said the victims included an elderly woman, two of her sons, a daughter-in-law, four grandchildren, and a two-year-old great-granddaughter. The Israeli military said it had targeted armed group members operating from the site and was reviewing reports of civilian harm.

A ceasefire announced last month has not stopped the fighting. Israeli airstrikes and drone activity continue day and night across southern Lebanon. Countless buildings have been destroyed, and most villages visited by a reporter over the past two weeks were nearly empty. Arab Salim, a village of around 6,000 before the war, now holds only about a tenth of its residents.

More than one million people — roughly one in five Lebanese — have been displaced, many still living in tents. Some residents who remain say they are too afraid to leave, and many say they have nowhere safe to go.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Displaced families across Lebanon sheltering amid continued airstrikes despite a ceasefire agreement

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Philippines — Manila
events.body:
Gunshots were fired inside the Philippine Senate building in Manila after military and police personnel entered the premises. A senator wanted by the International Criminal Court had taken refuge there, believing his arrest was imminent. Officials said no one was hurt, and no one has been arrested for the shooting. The government denied any intention to arrest the senator, saying troops were there to protect him.

The senator in question was police chief during a past president's war on drugs, in which thousands of alleged drug dealers were killed. That former president has been held in The Hague since March 2025 to face ICC proceedings. The senator's lawyers have appealed to the Supreme Court to block his extradition.

The Senate building remained locked down following the incident, with senators still inside. Protesters gathered outside, demanding the senator be sent to face trial. Officials are investigating who fired the shots and have called on the public to submit any video footage that could help.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Philippine senators and civilians amid gunfire, military presence, and political crisis inside the Senate building

events.title: Civil Unrest in Cuba — Havana
events.body:
Cuba has completely run out of diesel and fuel oil, according to the country's energy minister, leaving parts of Havana with blackouts lasting 20 to 22 hours a day. Hospitals have been unable to function normally, and schools and government offices have been forced to close. The minister described the energy situation as "critical" and the broader situation across the island as "extremely tense."

Hundreds of residents took to the streets in Havana on Wednesday evening in what was described as the largest single night of protests since the energy crisis began in January. People blocked roads with burning rubbish and shouted anti-government slogans. In one neighborhood, residents could be heard demanding the government restore electricity.

Cuba normally relies on Venezuela and Mexico for oil supplies, but both countries have largely stopped sending fuel after the United States threatened tariffs on nations that supply Cuba. The US has offered $100 million in humanitarian aid, tied to political reforms, while Cuba's government has blamed the energy shortages entirely on US pressure.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Millions of Cubans enduring severe fuel shortages, near-total daily blackouts, and widespread civil unrest

events.title: Armed Conflict in Congo 12/01/2024 — Uvira
events.body:
A human rights investigation has found evidence that rebel fighters and soldiers accused of being from Rwanda executed 53 civilians — including six children — during door-to-door raids in Uvira, a city in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. The killings took place after the M23 rebel group captured the city in December. Residents described watching family members shot in front of them.

Investigators also documented eight cases of rape during the occupation, with survivors describing threats at gunpoint. Other reported abuses included abductions, forced disappearances, and the forced recruitment of civilians. Investigators visited three suspected mass graves in the city, including one at a site previously used by UN peacekeepers.

The M23 withdrew from Uvira in January after diplomatic pressure. A separate United Nations report found more than 35,000 cases of sexual violence against children across eastern Congo in the first nine months of 2025. Nearly two million people in South Kivu province alone have fled their homes due to ongoing violence in the region.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in the Democratic Republic of Congo facing mass killings, sexual violence, and displacement amid armed rebel occupation

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ukraine 05/22/2025 — Kyiv
events.body:
At least seven people were killed, including a 12-year-old girl, after Russia launched a massive overnight wave of drone and missile strikes on Kyiv and other parts of Ukraine. Bodies were pulled from the rubble of a nine-storey apartment block in Kyiv's southwestern Darnytskyi district, where rescuers were still searching for around 20 people feared missing. Dozens more were wounded, including two children, and 21 people were being treated in hospital.

The barrage involved more than 670 drones and 56 missiles, hitting over 180 sites across Ukraine, including more than 50 residential buildings. Strikes also damaged a school, a veterinary clinic, and disrupted Kyiv's water supply. Officials said around 93% of the incoming drones and missiles were intercepted.

More than 1,500 rescuers and police officers were working across the country in the aftermath. The attack came shortly after a three-day ceasefire expired, and officials described it as one of the largest Russian strikes since the full-scale invasion began in 2022.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians across Ukraine killed and wounded after one of the largest drone and missile barrages since the 2022 invasion

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon 05/14/2026 — Srifa
events.body:
Israeli airstrikes killed two people in the town of Srifa in southern Lebanon, where warplanes hit a residential area. A drone strike near a vocational school between the towns of Breqa and Zrarieh injured one person, and another airstrike was reported in the Ain al-Tineh area of the Western Bekaa. The Israeli military also issued forced evacuation orders for at least eight towns and villages across southern Lebanon. Journalists described the past several days as one of the most intense periods of aerial bombardment in weeks, with many individual strikes targeting vehicles on the main coastal highway south of Beirut.

Lebanon's health ministry reported that at least 2,896 people have been killed in Israeli attacks since fighting resumed in early March. A Hezbollah drone also fell inside Israeli territory near the border, injuring several people. Representatives from Lebanon and Israel are set to meet in Washington to discuss extending a ceasefire scheduled to expire on Sunday, though Hezbollah has opposed the talks and is not participating.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians across Lebanon killed and displaced amid intense Israeli airstrikes and ongoing conflict

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon 05/14/2026 — Beirut
events.body:
Israel and Lebanon are holding a third round of direct talks aimed at preserving a ceasefire that is set to expire on Sunday. Previous meetings took place on April 14 and April 23, with delegations from both countries meeting face to face in Washington, D.C. The talks are deeply controversial inside Lebanon, with the president and prime minister supporting direct negotiations while Hezbollah and its allies prefer indirect talks.

Despite the ceasefire, Israeli attacks on Lebanon have continued. On Wednesday, eight people — including two children — were killed when drones struck vehicles on a road connecting southern Lebanon to Beirut. Nine more people were killed in strikes elsewhere in the country. On Thursday, additional strikes were carried out and forced evacuation orders were issued for towns in the eastern Bekaa Valley and the south.

Hezbollah has continued to respond with its own attacks, which the group says have targeted Israeli soldiers and vehicles on Lebanese soil, as well as some military sites in northern Israel.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Lebanon killed by drone strikes amid fragile ceasefire negotiations and continued cross-border violence

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ukraine — Kyiv
events.body:
A residential apartment building in Kyiv collapsed after being struck by Russian drones. Ukrainian officials say Russia launched more than 1,500 drones since Wednesday, causing damage in the capital despite a ceasefire proposal brokered by the United States.

The strikes came while a US-mediated ceasefire proposal was reportedly in place, raising serious concerns about civilian safety in urban areas. The scale of the drone campaign — over 1,500 launched in a short period — points to significant and ongoing risk for people living in cities and towns across Ukraine.

Residents in affected areas face continued danger from falling debris and building collapses, particularly in densely populated neighborhoods like those struck in Kyiv.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians across Ukraine facing mass drone strikes, building collapses, and ongoing danger amid ceasefire negotiations

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sudan — Darfur, Kordofan
events.body:
African Union and United Nations investigators have jointly warned that Sudan's ongoing war is widening into a major catastrophe involving ethnic cleansing, starvation, sexual violence, and attacks on civilians. A joint declaration issued during a human rights session in Banjul, The Gambia, says both the Rapid Support Forces and the Sudanese Armed Forces, along with allied groups, have committed serious violations — but that abuses by the Rapid Support Forces have been "particularly widespread and systematic."

Investigators expressed special alarm over conditions in Darfur, especially the El Fasher area, and the Kordofan regions, where civilians face siege conditions, forced displacement, and ethnically targeted violence. Hospitals, schools, markets, displacement camps, and aid workers have also been attacked, worsening hunger and collapsing basic services.

The declaration calls for an immediate ceasefire, unrestricted humanitarian access, and an inclusive civilian-led peace process. It urges cooperation with the International Criminal Court and proposes an African-led mechanism to prosecute war crimes. Sudan's official delegation rejected the statement.
events.source_title: dabangasudan.org
events.prayer: Millions of civilians in Sudan enduring ethnic violence, siege conditions, starvation, and systematic attacks on displacement camps and aid workers

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ukraine 05/14/2025 — Rivne
events.body:
Russia launched roughly 800 drones across Ukraine on Wednesday, killing at least six people and injuring many others. The attacks struck 14 regions, including Rivne, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson, Odesa, Kharkiv, and Dnipropetrovsk. A residential house was hit in Rivne, killing three people. An elderly man was killed in Zaporizhzhia when a guided bomb exploded nearby, and at least 20 people were wounded across Kherson. Russia also struck the far western region of Zakarpattia, near the Slovak border, prompting Slovakia to close the shared land border crossing indefinitely for security reasons.

The attacks came shortly after a three-day ceasefire brokered by the United States expired Monday night. Both sides reported violations during the truce, but no large aerial attacks occurred. Ukrainian military intelligence warned the current strikes could be prolonged, with massive missile attacks possibly following the drone waves.

Ukraine also struck targets inside Russia overnight, with officials reporting drone hits on industrial and energy facilities in several Russian regions. One person in Russia's Belgorod border region died from injuries sustained in a drone strike.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians across Ukraine killed and wounded as roughly 800 drones strike multiple regions following expired ceasefire

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon — Barja, Jiyeh, Saadiyat, Sidon, Arab Salim
events.body:
Twenty-two people, including eight children, were killed in Israeli air strikes across southern Lebanon, according to the country's health ministry. Drones struck three vehicles on a coastal highway near the towns of Barja, Jiyeh, and Saadiyat, about 20 kilometers from Beirut, killing eight people. Additional strikes hit cars near Sidon and several nearby towns, while later attacks killed more people in the villages of Arab Salim, Roumine, and Harouf.

The Israeli military said it targeted Hezbollah weapons storage sites and rocket launchers, and ordered residents of nine towns and villages to evacuate. Hezbollah said its fighters responded with drones, rockets, and explosives against Israeli troops in the south.

The Lebanese health ministry says more than 400 people have been killed across the country since a ceasefire was announced roughly a month ago. More than 10,600 homes have been damaged or destroyed in the same period. Israeli and Lebanese officials are expected to hold talks in Washington to try to stop the fighting.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Lebanon, including children, killed in airstrikes amid ongoing conflict and mass displacement

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon — southern Lebanon
events.body:
The United Nations peacekeeping force in southern Lebanon has raised concerns about a rise in drone activity near its positions, warning that the incidents are putting its personnel at risk. The force, known as UNIFIL, reported that explosions connected to suspected drone operations by Hezbollah and Israeli forces have been occurring close to its bases.

Officials said the activity is threatening what they described as already fragile stability in the region. UNIFIL did not specify exact locations but made clear the danger is affecting areas where its personnel are stationed throughout the south.

The warning reflects ongoing tension in the area, where a ceasefire has not fully settled the security situation on the ground. Civilians and aid workers in southern Lebanon continue to face uncertainty as armed activity persists near communities and UN positions alike.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: UN peacekeepers and civilians in Lebanon endangered by escalating drone activity amid fragile ceasefire conditions

events.title: Civil Unrest in South Africa — Durban
events.body:
Thousands of people in South Africa joined protests against illegal immigration, calling for the mass deportation of undocumented foreign nationals. Protesters say that undocumented immigrants have affected jobs, housing, and crime. South African authorities denied that attacks on foreigners had taken place, describing widely shared videos as fake, and the country's president called the unrest "isolated acts of criminality" that did not reflect government policy.

Ghana announced it would evacuate 300 of its citizens who registered with the embassy in Pretoria seeking to leave, describing them as distressed. The Ghanaian embassy also advised its nationals in Durban to avoid public gatherings and close their businesses ahead of a planned protest. Ghana and Nigeria both summoned South Africa's envoys over the treatment of their citizens, and Ghana asked the African Union to address the situation.

Kenya, Malawi, Lesotho, and Zimbabwe also warned their citizens in South Africa to take precautions. Xenophobia has been a recurring issue in the country, at times resulting in deadly violence.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Thousands of foreign nationals in South Africa facing xenophobic unrest, displacement, and fear amid mass protests

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ukraine 05/14/2025 — Dnipropetrovsk
events.body:
Thirteen people were killed and at least 41 injured in Russian drone and bomb strikes across Ukraine between Tuesday and Wednesday, shortly after a three-day ceasefire expired Monday night. The hardest-hit area was the Dnipropetrovsk region in central Ukraine, where eight people died and 11 were injured. Casualties were also reported in the Donetsk, Rivne, and Zaporizhzhia regions. In Kherson, nine people were wounded when a drone hit a passenger minibus.

Ukraine's air force said Russia launched 139 drones in a 24-hour period, with 111 shot down or intercepted and 20 direct hits recorded across 13 locations. Ukraine's president said 14 regions were struck and warned that missile strikes could follow. Railway infrastructure and civilian facilities were among the targets hit.

Russian officials reported that Ukrainian drones struck industrial sites in several Russian regions overnight, including a gas processing plant fire in the Astrakhan region, though no casualties were reported on the Russian side.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians across Ukraine killed and wounded in widespread drone and bomb strikes after ceasefire expiration

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon — Barja, Jiyeh, Saadiyat, Sidon, Maaliyeh, Chaaitiyeh, Naqoura
events.body:
At least 12 people were killed when Israeli forces struck seven cars in separate attacks south of Beirut, according to Lebanon's health ministry. Eight of those killed — including two children — died when drones hit three vehicles along a coastal highway near the towns of Barja, Jiyeh, and Saadiyat, roughly 20 kilometers from the capital. Four more were killed in strikes on cars in the city of Sidon and three nearby towns further south. The Israeli military did not comment directly on those strikes but said forces targeted Hezbollah infrastructure in several southern areas.

The attacks come despite a ceasefire announced nearly a month ago. Lebanon's health ministry says more than 400 people have been killed across the country since that ceasefire took effect, and at least 2,896 since the broader conflict began. Israeli and Lebanese officials are expected to hold talks in Washington aimed at halting the fighting, though Hezbollah's leadership has publicly stated it will not stop.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Lebanon, including children, killed in car strikes despite an active ceasefire agreement

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon 05/13/2026 — Jiyeh
events.body:
Three drone strikes hit cars on a major highway south of Beirut on Wednesday, killing at least eight people, including two children, according to Lebanon's Ministry of Health. The strikes occurred near Jiyeh, about 20 kilometers south of the capital. Photos from the scene showed vehicles charred and torn apart.

The Israeli military also issued forced displacement orders for six villages in southern Lebanon on the same day, warning residents to move at least one kilometer away or risk their lives. Among the areas affected is a displacement zone that contains one of only three remaining hospitals in the Tyre district. Aid workers say injured residents sometimes do not survive the long journey to reach care.

Lebanon's health ministry reported that at least 380 people have been killed since a ceasefire began, bringing the total death toll since March 2 to more than 2,800. More than one million people have been displaced across the country.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians across Lebanon killed, displaced, and cut off from hospital care amid ongoing drone strikes and forced evacuations

events.title: Displacement in Haiti — Cite Soleil, Port-au-Prince
events.body:
Residents of the Cite Soleil neighborhood in Port-au-Prince took to the streets Tuesday to demand government protection after a surge of gang violence over the weekend forced hundreds of people to flee their homes. Protesters said they had witnessed killings in the area in recent days. Haitian authorities have not released casualty figures.

Medical services in the area collapsed under the pressure. A major international medical organization evacuated its hospital after intense clashes on Sunday, and a second facility suspended all operations and moved out its patients, including 11 newborns.

Armed gangs now control an estimated 70 percent of the capital and have expanded looting, kidnapping, and sexual violence into rural areas. More than 1.4 million Haitians have already been displaced by gang warfare, with around 200,000 living in overcrowded sites in Port-au-Prince. A UN-authorized security mission has begun deploying, but its full contingent has not yet arrived.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Hundreds of thousands of displaced Haitians amid escalating gang violence, collapsed medical care, and mass displacement

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nigeria 05/10/2025 — Zamfara state
events.body:
The United Nations human rights chief has called for independent investigations after reports that air attacks by Nigerian and Chadian forces killed more than 100 civilians in northern Nigeria. Nigerian military airstrikes hit a market in Zamfara state on May 10, killing at least 100 civilians and injuring many more, according to the UN. Witnesses cited by Amnesty International said many of those killed were women and children at a crowded market in Tumfa village.

Separately, Chadian jets have been striking Boko Haram camps on remote islands in the Lake Chad basin since May 9. Those islands are shared marshland between Nigeria, Cameroon, Niger, and Chad. The bombardment reportedly killed dozens of Nigerian fishermen working in the area, and footage showed several survivors with severe burns being treated at a hospital across the border in Niger.

Nigeria's military denied that civilians were killed in Zamfara, saying the strike targeted a confirmed gathering of armed fighters. The UN urged both militaries to take precautions to protect civilians in any further operations.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Nigeria — including women, children, and fishermen — killed and injured in military airstrikes amid ongoing armed conflict

events.title: Armed Conflict in Palestine — Gaza
events.body:
Israeli attacks on Gaza increased by 35 percent in April compared to March, according to conflict-tracking group ACLED. Gaza's Ministry of Health reports that 120 Palestinians — including eight women and 13 children — were killed in Israeli strikes in the five weeks following a halt to joint US-Israel bombing of Iran on April 8. About 850 Palestinians have reportedly been killed since a ceasefire agreement took effect last October.

Israeli forces continue to occupy more than half of Gaza's territory, demolishing buildings and ordering residents to leave those areas. Aid restrictions remain in place, and rebuilding efforts have been blocked. More than two million people are now crowded into a narrow coastal strip, living in damaged structures or makeshift tents.

A resident whose son was killed in a late April strike said the war had not stopped in practice, despite official announcements. His family has been sheltering in the ruins of Khan Younis.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

events.title: Civil Unrest in Philippines 05/13/2026 — Philippine Senate, Manila
events.body:
Gunshots rang out at the Philippine Senate on Wednesday evening as soldiers and police moved in to arrest a sitting senator wanted by the International Criminal Court on charges of crimes against humanity. Journalists on the scene reported running for cover as about 15 shots were fired shortly after armed soldiers in protective gear entered the building. It was not immediately clear who fired. Security forces then ordered everyone to evacuate the building.

The senator, a former national police chief who oversaw the previous administration's "war on drugs," had been sheltering in his legislative office since Monday and publicly urged supporters to come to the Senate to block his arrest. The ICC unsealed the arrest warrant, dated last November, earlier this week. The same crimes have been charged against the former president he served, who is already in custody in The Hague awaiting trial.

The senator denied involvement in unlawful killings and appealed to the current president not to hand him over to the international court, saying he was willing to face justice inside the Philippines.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians, journalists, and officials in the Philippines amid gunfire and a tense ICC-related arrest at the national legislature

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon 05/13/2026 — Nabatieh
events.body:
Israeli strikes killed 13 people in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, including two rescue workers who responded to an earlier raid in the city of Nabatieh and a wounded person they had gone to help. Lebanon's health ministry condemned what it called a "complete disregard for all international norms." Hezbollah also carried out several attacks on Tuesday against Israeli forces operating inside south Lebanon and across the border, saying those actions were responses to Israeli ceasefire violations.

Lebanon's health minister stated that since a ceasefire took effect, 380 people have been killed and more than 1,100 wounded, including 39 women and 22 children. A children's rights organization reported that more than four children were killed or injured every day on average during the first 25 days of the truce. The overall death toll since the war began has reached 2,882, including more than 100 emergency and health workers.

Israeli troops continue to operate roughly 10 kilometers inside Lebanon and have issued evacuation warnings for dozens of southern and eastern villages ahead of ongoing strikes.
events.source_title: freemalaysiatoday.com
events.prayer: Civilians and rescue workers in Lebanon killed and wounded amid continuing strikes despite ceasefire agreement

events.title: Armed Conflict in Israel 10/07/2023 — Nova music festival, kibbutzim
events.body:
An independent Israeli investigation has released a 300-page report documenting what it calls "systematic, widespread" sexual violence carried out by Hamas and other Palestinian armed groups during the October 7, 2023 attacks. The report draws on more than 430 filmed interviews with survivors and witnesses, over 10,000 photographs and videos, and records from attack sites. It concludes that rapes, sexual assault, and sexual torture were used deliberately to cause maximum suffering.

The investigation covers attacks at the Nova music festival, where more than 370 people were killed, as well as at kibbutzim and military bases. Survivors and witnesses describe gang rapes, genital mutilation, and the killing of victims during or after assaults. The report also documents ongoing abuse of hostages held in captivity, affecting both women and men.

The commission describes these acts as war crimes, crimes against humanity, and genocidal acts under international law. Its evidence is being preserved in a secure archive and may be used in future prosecutions. Hamas has denied that any sexual violence took place.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Survivors and hostages in Israel carrying trauma from documented widespread sexual violence during the October 7 attacks

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon — Nabatieh
events.body:
Israeli air strikes killed at least 13 people across southern Lebanon, according to state media and Lebanon's health ministry. Six of those killed died overnight when a house was struck in Kfar Dounine. On Tuesday, two paramedics from Lebanon's Civil Defense emergency service were killed and a third was injured while responding to an earlier attack in Nabatieh that had already killed one person. A third strike killed three more people in Jabshit. A Syrian man was also killed when a drone hit a motorcycle in the Tayr Debba area.

Lebanon's health ministry accused Israeli forces of deliberately targeting the paramedics, calling it a violation of international humanitarian law. The ministry said that since the war began, more than 108 emergency and healthcare workers have been killed in Lebanon, with over 140 attacks recorded on ambulances and medical facilities.

Hezbollah launched drone attacks against Israeli troops in several areas and fired toward a community in northern Israel. Israeli and Lebanese officials are expected to meet in Washington on Thursday to discuss ending the conflict.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians, paramedics, and emergency workers across Lebanon killed amid ongoing airstrikes and repeated attacks on medical responders

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Qatar — Doha
events.body:
Qatar is stepping up diplomatic efforts to help end the ongoing war between the United States and Iran. The Qatari prime minister has held calls with officials in Iran, Saudi Arabia, Pakistan, Turkey, Kuwait, and others, and met with senior US officials including the vice president, secretary of state, and a White House envoy. Analysts say Qatar is playing a quiet but important behind-the-scenes role because it has working relationships with both Washington and Tehran.

Qatar has also been one of the countries hardest hit by the conflict. Iranian missiles and drones struck Qatar after US-Israeli strikes on Iran in late February, and a strike on Qatar's main gas facility forced a halt in production. Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz has cut off nearly all of Qatar's liquefied natural gas exports.

There are early signs the mediation may be having some effect. For the first time since the war began, two LNG tankers passed through or approached the Strait of Hormuz bound for Pakistan. Iran reportedly approved the transfers to help build confidence with its mediators.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Qatar's people and economy severely disrupted by regional war, missile strikes, and blocked gas exports

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Iran — Tehran
events.body:
Human rights advocates are raising urgent warnings about a sharp rise in executions and arrests inside Iran. Since a military conflict involving the United States and Israel began in February, more than 4,000 people have been detained on national-security charges, according to the United Nations human rights chief. At least 21 of those arrested have already been executed.

One monitoring group reports that Iran carried out at least 1,639 executions in 2025 — the highest number in 35 years, averaging four to five per day. Advocates say the government is moving faster and with less transparency on court cases involving political prisoners, meaning death sentences are being handed down and carried out more quickly.

Internet access has been significantly restricted since late February, making it harder for people inside Iran to communicate with the outside world. Human rights lawyers report that independent legal professionals have been arrested or intimidated, and that pressure on ill prisoners has increased. Advocates warn that past periods of political instability in Iran have been followed by mass executions.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Thousands of Iranian civilians detained, rapidly executed, and isolated amid escalating political repression

events.title: Serbia 05/12/2026 — Bujanovac
events.body:
Serbia and NATO have begun their first-ever joint military exercise, a two-week drill running through May 23 near Bujanovac in southern Serbia. About 600 troops from Serbia, Italy, Romania, and Türkiye are taking part, with military observers from several other countries also present. The exercise falls under NATO's Partnership for Peace programme, which Serbia has been part of for nearly 20 years.

The cooperation is notable given that NATO bombed Belgrade during the 1999 Kosovo war. Serbia has maintained a policy of military neutrality since then and is one of the few Balkan countries outside the alliance. Officials described the exercise as aimed at preserving regional peace and stability, and a NATO representative confirmed it fully respects Serbia's neutrality.

Serbia has not recognized Kosovo's independence, and a NATO-led peacekeeping force remains stationed there. The country continues to balance military ties with both NATO member states and Russia and China in its arms purchases.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Serbian civilians and military amid the country's first NATO joint exercise and ongoing regional security tensions

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ukraine 05/12/2026 — Orenburg
events.body:
Ukraine struck gas facilities in Russia's Orenburg region, more than 1,500 kilometers from the Ukrainian border, using drones. The attack came hours after Russia launched a large overnight strike on Ukraine involving more than 200 drones, killing six people in the Dnipropetrovsk region and damaging energy facilities, apartment buildings, a kindergarten, and a civilian train. Ukraine's president described the strike on Russia as retaliation and said his country would respond "symmetrically."

The attacks followed the end of a three-day ceasefire that had coincided with Russia's Victory Day holiday. Ukraine's foreign minister said Kyiv offered to extend the pause in fighting, but Russia refused. Meanwhile, Russia tested a new nuclear-capable long-range missile, which its president said would be deployed by year's end.

Talks aimed at ending the conflict have not produced a breakthrough. Ukrainian officials are reportedly worried that a new proposed ceasefire deal lacks the security guarantees Kyiv considers essential.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians across Ukraine killed and displaced as large-scale drone strikes damage homes, energy infrastructure, and public buildings

events.title: Displacement in Colombia — Norte de Santander
events.body:
A new report from the International Committee of the Red Cross says 2025 brought the worst humanitarian conditions in a decade for civilians caught in Colombia's long-running conflict. The number of people individually displaced by fighting more than doubled to over 235,000, with nearly half of those cases in the department of Norte de Santander. Mass displacement events also doubled, affecting more than 87,000 people.

The report also found a sharp rise in the use of explosives and drones. Nearly 1,000 people were killed or injured by explosive devices in 2025, an increase of more than a third from the previous year. Fear of violence has pushed many families to stay home, cutting off access to schools, farmland, and basic services. The number of communities experiencing lockdowns amid armed group fighting nearly doubled.

The Red Cross says this deterioration has been building since 2018 and has weakened community ties and altered daily life in lasting ways.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Over 320,000 civilians in Colombia displaced amid the worst conflict conditions in a decade

events.title: Civil Unrest in Argentina 05/13/2026 — Argentina
events.body:
Large crowds took to the streets in major cities across Argentina to protest cuts to the country's public university system. Demonstrators called on the president to follow an existing university funding law and reverse budget reductions affecting the tuition-free institutions.

The protests reflect ongoing tension over the government's austerity measures, which critics say are starving public universities of needed resources. No reports of violence were mentioned in the source.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Students and citizens across Argentina protesting severe budget cuts threatening the public university system

events.title: Armed Conflict in Palestine — West Bank
events.body:
United Nations agencies are warning that growing numbers of Palestinian children in the occupied West Bank are being killed or seriously injured. The violence involves both military operations and attacks by settlers, with children reported to have been shot, stabbed, and pepper-sprayed.

In Gaza, the situation for children with serious injuries is described as critical. Tens of thousands are living with life-changing injuries but cannot access the treatment or rehabilitation they need.

The warnings were issued on a Tuesday, though no specific date for the underlying events was given in the source. UN agencies say the trend is worsening, leaving many children without medical care at a time when their injuries require urgent and ongoing attention.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories

events.title: Armed Conflict in Pakistan 05/13/2025 — Sarai Naurang
events.body:
A bomb hidden in a rickshaw exploded in a busy market in Sarai Naurang, a town in the Lakki Marwat district of northwestern Pakistan, killing at least nine people and wounding around 30 others. Those killed include two traffic police officers and a woman. Dozens of injured were taken to hospitals in the nearby city of Bannu, with some in critical condition.

No group immediately claimed responsibility. The attack came just days after 21 police officers were killed in a bombing and gun assault on a security post in Bannu district — an attack Pakistani officials blamed on the Pakistan Taliban (TTP). The TTP is a separate but allied group to Afghanistan's ruling Taliban and has increased attacks on Pakistani security forces in recent years.

Pakistan has long accused Afghanistan's Taliban government of sheltering TTP fighters, a charge Kabul denies. Cross-border tensions have been high since late February, when fighting killed hundreds of people on both sides of the border.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians and security forces in Pakistan wounded and killed by a market bombing amid rising militant attacks

events.title: Armed Conflict in Israel — West Bank
events.body:
Since the start of 2025, at least one Palestinian child has been killed every week on average in the occupied West Bank, according to the United Nations. The deaths have been caused by both Israeli military forces and settlers.

A UNICEF spokesperson publicly condemned the rising death toll and called on Israeli authorities to uphold the rights of children. The statement reflects growing UN concern about the pace and scale of child casualties in the territory.

The consistent weekly rate of child deaths points to an ongoing pattern of violence affecting civilian families across the West Bank.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Palestinian children in the West Bank killed at a consistent weekly rate amid ongoing armed violence

events.title: Indonesia 05/02/2025 — Mount Dukono, Halmahera
events.body:
A volcano on Halmahera island in North Maluku erupted while a group of 20 hikers was near the summit, killing three people and injuring others. Two of those killed were Singaporean and one was Indonesian. The remaining hikers were evacuated and treated at a nearby hospital.

Indonesian authorities said they had banned climbing permits for the mountain since April 17 and had restricted access within 4 kilometers of the crater. Warnings had also been posted on social media and at trail entrances. The guide leading the group said he was unaware of the prohibition when the hike began.

The volcano had erupted more than 200 times since late March. Rescue teams recovered the bodies of all three victims over the weekend, with workers digging through thick volcanic ash beneath a large rock to retrieve remains. Surviving Singaporean hikers have since returned home.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Hikers in Indonesia caught in a volcanic eruption, leaving three dead and others injured

events.title: Israel — Jerusalem
events.body:
Israel's parliament passed a law allowing the death penalty and public broadcast trials for people accused of involvement in the October 7, 2023 Hamas-led attacks. The vote passed 93 to 0, with rare joint support from both government and opposition lawmakers. The law creates a special military court in Jerusalem where key moments — including opening statements, verdicts, and sentencing — will be filmed and shared publicly online.

Those facing prosecution include captured members of Hamas's special forces unit. Charges are expected to range from terrorism and murder to sexual violence and genocide, with genocide carrying the death penalty. Israel currently holds over 1,200 people as unlawful combatants without formal charges, most from Gaza.

Israeli human rights groups have spoken out against the law, raising concerns about capital punishment and warning that confessions may have been obtained under torture. Families of October 7 victims participated in drafting the legislation and have asked that sensitive information be shared with them before any public release.
events.source_title: BBC News

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Philippines — Manila
events.body:
A Filipino senator who served as national police chief during a deadly anti-drug campaign fled into the Senate building as investigators tried to arrest him, hours before an International Criminal Court warrant for his arrest was made public. Security camera footage showed agents chasing him through the building's stairwells and corridors before he reached safety. Officials later said they would not arrest him while he remained under the Senate's protection.

The senator faces ICC accusations of being an indirect participant in the killing of at least 32 people between 2016 and 2018. The former president who launched that campaign has been held at The Hague since March 2025. The senator's lawyers have asked the Supreme Court to block his arrest, and he has urged supporters gathered outside the building to wait there until the court rules.

The standoff is unfolding against a backdrop of sharp political rivalry between two powerful political families, with the vice president facing a second impeachment vote and a Senate now led by allies of the detained former president.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Philippines gripped by political standoff as ICC arrest warrant targets former national police chief

events.title: Armed Conflict in Afghanistan 03/16/2025 — Kabul
events.body:
A Pakistani airstrike on a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul killed at least 269 people on the evening of March 16, making it the deadliest single attack in Afghanistan's recent history. The United Nations, which visited the site, confirmed the toll but said the real number is likely higher. Three bombs struck the Omid Drug Rehabilitation Hospital along the Kabul-Jalalabad highway around 8:50 p.m. local time. Many victims were burned beyond recognition or reduced to body parts.

The UN and Human Rights Watch say the strike hit civilians receiving treatment, and there are calls for an investigation into the attack as a war crime. Pakistan denies hitting a civilian target, saying it struck "military and terrorist infrastructure." Families of more than 30 victims, including those of recovering addicts and staff, reject that claim.

The attack comes amid months of cross-border fighting between Pakistan and Afghanistan, with Islamabad accusing the Taliban government of sheltering militants. The rehabilitation centre had been open since 2016 and was well known to international agencies operating nearby.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Hundreds of civilians killed in Afghanistan after a deadly airstrike struck a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul

events.title: Coup/Crisis in United States
events.body:
A ceasefire between the United States and Iran that has been in place for roughly a month is in serious danger of collapsing. The US president described the ceasefire as "unbelievably weak" and called Iran's peace proposal "totally unacceptable," while Iran's parliamentary speaker warned that Iranian forces were ready to respond to any aggression.

Iran's counter-proposal reportedly calls for an immediate end to fighting on all fronts, a halt to the US naval blockade of Iranian ports, compensation for war damage, and recognition of Iranian control over the Strait of Hormuz. The US rejected the terms. A dispute also emerged over enriched uranium, with the US claiming Iran had agreed to allow its removal and Iran denying any such agreement was made.

The Strait of Hormuz remains blocked by Iran, pushing world oil prices higher. About 20% of the world's oil and gas normally passes through the waterway, meaning a continued standoff has real consequences for fuel costs around the world.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Fragile US-Iran ceasefire at risk of collapse amid disputed terms and blocked oil shipping lanes

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sudan — Sudan
events.body:
Armed drones were responsible for more than 80 percent of civilian deaths in Sudan's ongoing war during the first four months of 2026, according to the United Nations human rights chief. At least 880 civilians were killed in that period, with drones identified as the leading cause.

The UN official warned that the growing use of drone warfare could push the conflict into an even deadlier phase. The statement was delivered on a Monday, though no specific location within Sudan was named as the site of a particular strike.

The figures highlight the severe danger facing ordinary people across the country, where the war has already caused widespread suffering. The warning from UN leadership signals that conditions for civilians could worsen significantly if drone use continues to escalate.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Civilians across Sudan killed in growing drone strikes amid escalating armed conflict

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon — Lebanon
events.body:
The United Nations reported on a Monday that conditions for civilians in Lebanon continue to worsen, even after a ceasefire with Israel was announced the previous month. Despite the halt in formal fighting, the humanitarian situation has not improved as hoped.

Violence also continued in Gaza over the same period, according to the UN report, which described the weekend as deadly across the broader region.

The UN's assessment highlights that ceasefire agreements do not always bring immediate relief for people on the ground. Civilians in Lebanon are still facing hardship, and those in Gaza remain in danger from ongoing violence.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories

events.title: Displacement in Solomon Islands — Solomon Islands
events.body:
Tropical Cyclone Maila has affected more than 150,000 people across the Solomon Islands, adding to what United Nations relief coordinators describe as "significant humanitarian needs" in the country. The storm is the latest in a series of weather events to strike the Pacific region.

Beyond the immediate storm damage, the emergency has been complicated by the presence of hidden explosive remnants from past conflict, which pose an ongoing danger to displaced residents and relief workers moving through affected areas. The combination of storm damage, displacement, and these hidden hazards has deepened the scale of the crisis.

UN relief coordination office OCHA is tracking the situation. Civilians who have been forced from their homes face risks not only from the destruction left by the cyclone, but also from unexploded ordnance that can be difficult to detect in areas disrupted by the storm.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Over 150,000 people in the Solomon Islands displaced by a cyclone and endangered by hidden unexploded ordnance

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ukraine 05/12/2026 — Dnipropetrovsk
events.body:
A three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine ended on Tuesday, and Russia launched more than 200 long-range drones at Ukraine overnight. At least one person was killed and four others were wounded in attacks on the Dnipropetrovsk region. Drones also struck energy infrastructure in the Mykolaiv region, causing power outages, and hit residential buildings and a kindergarten in the Kyiv region. Additional attacks were reported in the Kharkiv, Zhytomyr, Sumy, and Chernihiv regions.

The truce, brokered by the United States, had run from May 9 to 11 and coincided with Russia's Victory Day holiday. Before it even expired, both sides accused the other of violations. Russia's government confirmed it was resuming its offensive, saying the ceasefire had simply ended.

Ukraine also launched drone attacks, with Russia reporting it shot down 27 Ukrainian drones over its Belgorod, Voronezh, and Rostov regions. Peace talks have made little progress, and civilians on both sides remain at risk as large-scale aerial exchanges continue.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians across Ukraine facing renewed large-scale drone strikes after a collapsed ceasefire, with energy and residential areas hit

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon 05/12/2026 — Kfar Dounin
events.body:
An Israeli air strike on a house in the Kfar Dounin municipality in southern Lebanon killed six people and wounded seven others, who were taken to hospitals in the coastal city of Tyre.
